## Latest CHANGELOG Entry

## [Unreleased] -- 2026-05-18 (SAN-666)

### Changed

- **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** (Verify golden receipts step in cross-sdk-smoke-python job): broadened tampered-fixture glob from `"999_tampered"*` to `*tampered*` to match sanna-repo's CI script (which was broadened by SAN-533 when per-cv tampered fixtures were added at golden/receipts/v{cv}_tampered.json for cv in {5, 6, 7, 8, 10}). Added 4-line explanatory comment block above the for loop documenting both tampered conventions (999_tampered* legacy + v{cv}_tampered* per-cv), matching the comment block in sanna-repo/.github/workflows/ci.yml.

### Why this matters

The cross-SDK gate in sanna-protocol (added by SAN-491) is intended to mirror sanna-repo's CI script for cross-SDK consumption smoke testing. SAN-533 added per-cv tampered fixtures and broadened sanna-repo's tampered detection glob to `*tampered*`; the sanna-protocol mirror script was not updated in lockstep. The drift was masked until SAN-665 landed the sanna-repo schema mirror update, allowing SAN-647's cross-sdk-smoke-python re-run to pass the diff -q gate and reach the Verify golden receipts step, which then failed because per-cv tampered fixtures fell through to the expect-to-pass branch. This fix restores the script-mirror invariant so additive-schema protocol PRs (like SAN-647) can pass cross-SDK CI cleanly.
